items.positivePrompt: Original fictional Advanced OT Cybersecurity Engineer for an offshore-wind strategy card game; full-body character key art, androgynous-presenting adult analyst, narrow face, cool brown skin tone, undercut hairstyle, slim build, looking back over shoulder while carrying a rugged laptop, cautious expression, backlit deck perspective, head-to-toe visible; 警覺、紀律、攻防思考; carrying zero-trust network map and security key; professional technical attire with task-appropriate PPE, institution-free insignia; electric blue, violet, dark graphite; Taiwan-Strait-inspired maritime environment; cinematic semi-realistic anime aesthetic with luminous atmospheric lighting, naturalistic faces, meticulous industrial detail, realistic proportions and PPE; vertical 2:3 full-bleed composition; keep face, hands and essential tools inside a 12 percent safe zone; source artwork only, designed for a separate runtime card-frame overlay; no resemblance to any real person or institution. Character direction is not gender-locked: create one original fictional adult offshore-wind professional; visual diversity is mandatory for this character: androgynous-presenting adult analyst, narrow face, cool brown skin tone, undercut hairstyle, slim build, looking back over shoulder while carrying a rugged laptop, cautious expression, backlit deck perspective; credible task-appropriate PPE and a non-sexualized presentation; do not reuse the same face or default front-facing pose from earlier P01 art. Engineering background accuracy is mandatory. Every visible offshore wind turbine must be a conventional modern three-bladed horizontal-axis wind turbine. Each complete rotor has exactly three smooth, tapered aerodynamic blades attached to one centered hub and evenly spaced 120 degrees apart. Blade curvature must remain continuous and structurally plausible, with consistent length, taper, perspective and attachment at the blade roots. The nacelle must sit correctly on one tower with a plausible offshore foundation and access platform. Keep rotors visually separated from the character and from other turbines. Prefer a few clearly readable turbines; if a rotor would be heavily occluded or cropped, omit that turbine rather than inventing partial geometry.
items.negativePrompt: card border, decorative frame, title, subtitle, text, letters, numbers, icon, skill badge, rarity badge, logo, watermark, HUD, UI, health bar, fatigue meter, status bar, institution emblem, company brand, real-person likeness, celebrity likeness, copied artist signature, cropped head, cropped hands, extra fingers, duplicate limbs, malformed tools, unsafe PPE, same face, repeated face template, identical facial proportions, identical hairstyle, identical neutral confident stance, repeated front-facing pose, idol pin-up pose, child, minor, teenage schoolgirl, school uniform, childlike body, infantilized face, sexualized character, revealing PPE, fashion outfit replacing safety equipment, pin-up pose, two-bladed wind turbine, four-bladed wind turbine, five-bladed wind turbine, extra turbine blade, missing turbine blade, duplicate turbine blade, fused blades, branching blade, forked blade, partially formed rotor, asymmetric rotor, uneven blade spacing, off-center hub, detached blade, floating blade, broken blade, bent blade, kinked blade, warped blade, twisted blade, melted blade, rubber-like blade, inconsistent blade length, malformed blade root, blade passing through hub, blade intersecting nacelle, blade intersecting tower, blade intersecting platform, blade intersecting character, blades intersecting each other, overlapping turbines, malformed nacelle, duplicate nacelle, duplicated tower, split tower, leaning tower, floating turbine, impossible offshore foundation, impossible ladder, impossible access platform, inconsistent turbine scale, impossible perspective, turbine growing out of another structure

characterDiversityPolicy.appliesTo: Pending and future P01 source-art generations only; generated R3 art remains unchanged unless explicitly regenerated.
characterDiversityPolicy.validation: tools/validate-art-prompts.mjs checks every pending batch for 3 masculine, 2 androgynous, <=5 feminine, and at least five unique pose silhouettes.
